metadata_information.title: How can Indonesia maintain creditworthiness and noninflationary growth
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: How can Indonesia maintain creditworthiness and noninflationary growth

document_description.abstract: Despite external shocks, Indonesia has maintained creditworthiness through swift adjustment. Indonesia's flexible economic management and clear policy signals have lent stability to the economy, in contrast to the stop and go reforms, uncertainty, and constant debt renegotiations in many high debt countries. The authors use an econometrically estimated macroeconomic model to analyze open economy adjustment in Indonesia - particularly the interaction between the exchange rate, the interest rate, growth, and debt - and to analyze future policy changes in light of Indonesia's objectives for growth, external debt, and inflation.
